Name: UPrimitiveComponent
Description:
PrimitiveComponents are SceneComponents that contain or generate some sort of geometry, generally to be rendered or used as collision data.
  There are several subclasses for the various types of geometry, but the most common by far are the ShapeComponents (Capsule, Sphere, Box), StaticMeshComponent, and SkeletalMeshComponent.
  ShapeComponents generate geometry that is used for collision detection but are not rendered, while StaticMeshComponents and SkeletalMeshComponents contain pre-built geometry that is rendered, but can also be used for collision detection.

Variables.Name: ExpectedQualityLimit
Variables.Description: If limit > actual, primitive won't be rendered.

Variables.Name: bFixedLODDistanceFactorSwitch
Variables.Description:
open this switch to use r.LOD.FixedDistanceFactor to control lod switch
	 for example r.LOD.FixedDistanceFactor=0.5 is half distance of origin to switch new lod

Variables.Name: CullingScreenSize
Variables.Description:
If the screen percentage of the bounding box under this value, it will be culled.
	 Set "0" to avoid contribution culling

Variables.Name: MinDrawDistance
Variables.Description:
The minimum distance at which the primitive should be rendered,
	  measured in world space units from the center of the primitive's bounding sphere to the camera position.

Variables.Name: LDMaxDrawDistance
Variables.Description: Max draw distance exposed to LDs. The real max draw distance is the min (disregarding 0) of this and volumes affecting this object.

Variables.Name: CachedMaxDrawDistance
Variables.Description:
The distance to cull this primitive at.
	  A CachedMaxDrawDistance of 0 indicates that the primitive should not be culled by distance.

Variables.Name: DepthPriorityGroup
Variables.Description: The scene depth priority group to draw the primitive in.

Variables.Name: ViewOwnerDepthPriorityGroup
Variables.Description: The scene depth priority group to draw the primitive in, if it's being viewed by its owner.

Variables.Name: LightmapType
Variables.Description: Controls the type of lightmap used for this component.

Variables.Name: VLMOptimizeType
Variables.Description: To optimize performance, VLM can select optimization method.

Variables.Name: bRenderToTerrainVirtualTexture
Variables.Description: This primitive will be rendered to terrain VT if true

Variables.Name: bForceInjectToHierarchicalSurfel
Variables.Description:
------------------------------------Surfel GI Begin------------------------------------
	 If true, the primitive intersecting with the surfel volume will be injected into the volume whenever the camera moves.

Variables.Name: bForceUseStaticMovability
Variables.Description: If true, the movability of the primitive will be considered as static in Surfel GI pipeline.

Variables.Name: bAffectSurfelGIWhenHidden
Variables.Description: If true, always affect global illumination even if hidden in game

Variables.Name: bBulletCanBreakThrough
Variables.Description: 子弹碰撞穿透

Variables.Name: bAlwaysCreatePhysicsState
Variables.Description:
Indicates if we'd like to create physics state all the time (for collision and simulation).
	  If you set this to false, it still will create physics state if collision or simulation activated.
	  This can help performance if you'd like to avoid overhead of creating physics state when triggers

Variables.Name: bGenerateOverlapEvents
bUseSingleSweep
Variables.Description: Use Sweep or single trace

Variables.Name: bMultiBodyOverlap
Variables.Description:
If true, this component will generate individual overlaps for each overlapping physics body if it is a multi-body component. When false, this component will
	  generate only one overlap, regardless of how many physics bodies it has and how many of them are overlapping another componentbody. This flag has no
	  influence on single body components.

Variables.Name: bCheckAsyncSceneOnMove
bTraceComplexOnMove
bReturnMaterialOnMove
Variables.Description:
If true, component sweeps will return the material in their hit result.
	  @see MoveComponent(), FHitResult

Variables.Name: bUseViewOwnerDepthPriorityGroup
Variables.Description: True if the primitive should be rendered using ViewOwnerDepthPriorityGroup if viewed by its owner.

Variables.Name: bAllowCullDistanceVolume
Variables.Description: Whether to accept cull distance volumes to modify cached cull distance.

Variables.Name: bHasMotionBlurVelocityMeshes
Variables.Description: true if the primitive has motion blur velocity meshes

Variables.Name: bVisibleInReflectionCaptures
Variables.Description: If true, this component will be visible in reflection captures.

Variables.Name: bRejectReflectionCapture
Variables.Description: If true, this component won't be affected by any reflection capture.

Variables.Name: bRenderInMainPass
Variables.Description: If true, this component will be rendered in the main pass (z prepass, basepass, transparency)

Variables.Name: bForceRenderInShadowPass
Variables.Description: If true, this component will force be rendered in the shadow depth pass when bRenderInMainPass is false

Variables.Name: HiddenInMainPassLocks
Variables.Description: If Num() == 0, this component will be rendered in the main pass (z prepass, basepass, transparency)

Variables.Name: bRenderInMono
Variables.Description: If true, this component will be rendered in mono only if an HMD is connected and monoscopic far field rendering is activated.

Variables.Name: bReceivesDecals
Variables.Description: Whether the primitive receives decals.

Variables.Name: bOwnerNoSee
Variables.Description: If this is True, this component won't be visible when the view actor is the component's owner, directly or indirectly.

Variables.Name: bOnlyOwnerSee
Variables.Description: If this is True, this component will only be visible when the view actor is the component's owner, directly or indirectly.

Variables.Name: bTreatAsBackgroundForOcclusion
Variables.Description: Treat this primitive as part of the background for occlusion purposes. This can be used as an optimization to reduce the cost of rendering skyboxes, large ground planes that are part of the vista, etc.

Variables.Name: bDrawIdeaOutline
Variables.Description: Whether to render the primitive's outline

Variables.Name: bIdeaOutlineUseNormalInVertexColor
Variables.Description: Whether to use normal vector stored in vertex color

Variables.Name: bIdeaOutlineNew
Variables.Description:
Should only be used in UGC and Home branch for now. This may significantly increase outline cost. Be sure you need this feature before you enable it. 
	 Whether to use new outline pass.

Variables.Name: bIdeaOutlineOcclusionHighlight
Variables.Description: Whether to use occlusion highlight

Variables.Name: bDisableWriteDepthForOcclusionHighlight
Variables.Description: Whether to occlude other primitive's highlight. if this is already occlude highlight, it won't write depth and this flag make no use.

Variables.Name: bIdeaOutlineNewUseBackFace
Variables.Description: use backface for outline drawing in outline pass

Variables.Name: bIdeaOverrideOutlineAndOcclusion
Variables.Description: Override outline settings to enable both outline and occlusion

Variables.Name: bDrawIdeaOutlineInHighlightPass
Variables.Description: Move old draw outline to highlight pass, not work for outline for separate pass, maybe custom depth outline in the future

Variables.Name: IdeaOutlineOcclusionColor
Variables.Description: Edit it when enable Use Both Outline And Occlusion, otherwise use IdeaOutlineColor

Variables.Name: bOverrideIdeaOutlineColor
Variables.Description: Whether to override the primitive's outline color

Variables.Name: bOverrideIdeaOutlineThickness
Variables.Description: Whether to override the primitive's outline color

Variables.Name: IdeaOutlineThickness
Variables.Description: the primitive's override outline color

Variables.Name: IdeaOutlineColor
Variables.Description: the primitive's override outline color

Variables.Name: bDrawHighlight
Variables.Description: Whether to draw highlight for this primitive

Variables.Name: bHighlightCanBeOccluded
Variables.Description: Whether the highlight mesh of this primitive can be occluded

Variables.Name: bOverrideHighlightColor
Variables.Description: Whether to use HighlightColor for highlight rendering, if false, use the default color in HighlightMaterial

Variables.Name: HighlightColor
Variables.Description: If bOverrideHighlightColor is true, use this color for highlight rendering

Variables.Name: DrawDyeingMode
Variables.Description: Draw dyeing mode of primitive

Variables.Name: VisibleDyeingColor
Variables.Description: Primitive's visible color when dyeing

Variables.Name: OccludedDyeingColor
Variables.Description: Primitive's occlued color when dyeing

Variables.Name: bDrawDyeing
Variables.Description: Whether to dyeing the primitive

Variables.Name: bUseAsEarlyZ
Variables.Description: Whether to render the primitive in the early z pass for mobile platform.

Variables.Name: bRenderInTwoPass
Variables.Description:
Whether to render the primitive in the early z pass for mobile platform.   
	 If the mesh is visibility grid's proxy  
	 Whether to render the primitive in two pass - only work on masked hair model

Variables.Name: bTwoPassTranslucent
Variables.Description: Whether to render translucency in two pass.

Variables.Name: bTranslucentDepthWrite
Variables.Description: Whether to write depth for translucency.

Variables.Name: bTranslucentDepthWriteInTwoPass
Variables.Description: Write depth for translucency in two pass. Add a depth-only pass before rendering the translucent object.

Variables.Name: bForceIBL
Variables.Description: (TAPD:ID869829499) for SceneProxyIBL

Variables.Name: bIsTppLayer
Variables.Description:
When enabled, the component will NOT cast a shadow on components with bIsFppLayer enabled.
	  This requires bCastInsetShadow to be enabled.

Variables.Name: bUseAsOccluder
bSelectable
Variables.Description: If this is True, this component can be selected in the editor.

Variables.Name: bForceMipStreaming
Variables.Description: If true, forces mips for textures used by this component to be resident when this component's level is loaded.

Variables.Name: bHasPerInstanceHitProxies
Variables.Description: If true a hit-proxy will be generated for each instance of instanced static meshes

Variables.Name: bRecieveShadow
Variables.Description: Controls whether the primitive component should recieve a shadow or not.(by jinglei)

Variables.Name: CastShadow
Variables.Description:
Controls whether the primitive component should cast a shadow or not.
	 
	  This flag is ignored (no shadows will be generated) if all materials on this component have an Unlit shading model.

Variables.Name: bAffectDynamicIndirectLighting
Variables.Description: Controls whether the primitive should inject light into the Light Propagation Volume.  This flag is only used if CastShadow is true.

Variables.Name: bAffectDistanceFieldLighting
Variables.Description: Controls whether the primitive should affect dynamic distance field lighting methods.  This flag is only used if CastShadow is true.

Variables.Name: bCastDynamicShadow
Variables.Description: Controls whether the primitive should cast shadows in the case of non precomputed shadowing.  This flag is only used if CastShadow is true.

Variables.Name: bCastStaticShadow
Variables.Description: Whether the object should cast a static shadow from shadow casting lights.  This flag is only used if CastShadow is true.

Variables.Name: bCastVolumetricTranslucentShadow
Variables.Description:
Whether the object should cast a volumetric translucent shadow.
	  Volumetric translucent shadows are useful for primitives `with smoothly changing opacity like particles representing a volume,
	  But have artifacts when used on highly opaque surfaces.

Variables.Name: bSelfShadowOnly
Variables.Description:
When enabled, the component will only cast a shadow on itself and not other components in the world.
	  This is especially useful for first person weapons, and forces bCastInsetShadow to be enabled.

Variables.Name: bCastFarShadow
Variables.Description: When enabled, the component will be rendering into the far shadow cascades (only for directional lights).

Variables.Name: bCastInDoorShadow
Variables.Description: When enabled, the component will be rendering shadow in door (only for directional lights).

Variables.Name: bCastInsetShadow
Variables.Description:
Whether this component should create a per-object shadow that gives higher effective shadow resolution.
	  Useful for cinematic character shadowing. Assumed to be enabled if bSelfShadowOnly is enabled.

Variables.Name: bCastPhotonShadow
Variables.Description: #if WITH_PHOTON_SHADOW

Variables.Name: bCastPhotonPerObjectShadow
Variables.Description: #if WITH_PHOTON_PER_OBEJCT_SHADOW

Variables.Name: bCastCinematicShadow
Variables.Description:
Whether this component should cast shadows from lights that have bCastShadowsFromCinematicObjectsOnly enabled.
	  This is useful for characters in a cinematic with special cinematic lights, where the cost of shadowmap rendering of the environment is undesired.

Variables.Name: bCastHiddenShadow
Variables.Description:
If true, the primitive will cast shadows even if bHidden is true.
	 	Controls whether the primitive should cast shadows when hidden.
	 	This flag is only used if CastShadow is true.

Variables.Name: bCastShadowAsTwoSided
Variables.Description: Whether this primitive should cast dynamic shadows as if it were a two sided material.

Variables.Name: bLightAttachmentsAsGroup
Variables.Description:
Whether to light this component and any attachments as a group.  This only has effect on the root component of an attachment tree.
	  When enabled, attached component shadowing settings like bCastInsetShadow, bCastVolumetricTranslucentShadow, etc, will be ignored.
	  This is useful for improving performance when multiple movable components are attached together.

Variables.Name: bReceiveCombinedCSMAndStaticShadowsFromStationaryLights
Variables.Description:
Mobile only:
	  If enabled this component can receive combined static and CSM shadows from a stationary light. (Enabling will increase shading cost.)
	  If disabled this component will only receive static shadows from stationary lights.

Variables.Name: bSingleSampleShadowFromStationaryLights
Variables.Description:
Whether the whole component should be shadowed as one from stationary lights, which makes shadow receiving much cheaper.
	  When enabled shadowing data comes from the volume lighting samples precomputed by Lightmass, which are very sparse.
	  This is currently only used on stationary directional lights.

Variables.Name: bIgnoreRadialImpulse
Variables.Description: Will ignore radial impulses applied to this component.

Variables.Name: bIgnoreRadialForce
Variables.Description: Will ignore radial forces applied to this component.

Variables.Name: bApplyImpulseOnDamage
Variables.Description: True for damage to this component to apply physics impulse, false to opt out of these impulses.

Variables.Name: bReplicatePhysicsToAutonomousProxy
Variables.Description:
True if physics should be replicated to autonomous proxies. This should be true for
		server-authoritative simulations, and false for client authoritative simulations.

Variables.Name: AlwaysLoadOnClient
Variables.Description: If this is True, this component must always be loaded on clients, even if Hidden and CollisionEnabled is NoCollision.

Variables.Name: AlwaysLoadOnServer
Variables.Description: If this is True, this component must always be loaded on servers, even if Hidden and CollisionEnabled is NoCollision

Variables.Name: bUseEditorCompositing
Variables.Description: Composite the drawing of this component onto the scene after post processing (only applies to editor drawing)

Variables.Name: bRenderCustomDepth
Variables.Description: If true, this component will be rendered in the CustomDepth pass (usually used for outlines)

Variables.Name: bUseAsyncCompilePSO
Variables.Description:
#if WITH_ANDROID_ASYNC_COMPILE_PSO
	 whether this mesh is using async compile pso , only used for android

Variables.Name: bMoveMultiPenetratingIgnoreFlag
Variables.Description: 是否在移动的时候，有多个渗透，就忽略开启本标志的物体

Variables.Name: bHasCustomNavigableGeometry
Variables.Description: If true then DoCustomNavigableGeometryExport will be called to collect navigable geometry of this component.

Variables.Name: CanCharacterStepUpOn
JumpOffVelocityFactor
Variables.Description:
不能站的时候，角色随机移动的最大速度的比率
	 如果>0，表示使用本值，移动组件上的值无效；否则使用移动组件上的值

Variables.Name: LightingChannels
Variables.Description:
Channels that this component should be in.  Lights with matching channels will affect the component.
	  These channels only apply to opaque materials, direct lighting, and dynamic lighting and shadowing.

Variables.Name: CustomDepthStencilWriteMask
Variables.Description: Mask used for stencil buffer writes.

Variables.Name: CustomDepthStencilValue
Variables.Description: Optionally write this 0-255 value to the stencil buffer in CustomDepth pass (Requires project setting or r.CustomDepth == 3)

Variables.Name: TranslucencySortPriority
Variables.Description:
Translucent objects with a lower sort priority draw behind objects with a higher priority.
	  Translucent objects with the same priority are rendered from back-to-front based on their bounds origin.
	 
	  Ignored if the object is not translucent.  The default priority is zero.
	  Warning: This should never be set to a non-default value unless you know what you are doing, as it will prevent the renderer from sorting correctly.
	  It is especially problematic on dynamic gameplay effects.

Variables.Name: TerrainRVTRenderSortPriority
Variables.Description:
Objects with a lower sort priority draw behind objects with a higher priority.
	  Objects with the same priority are rendered from back-to-front based on their bounds origin.

Variables.Name: VisibilityId
Variables.Description: Used for precomputed visibility

Variables.Name: PVSHandlerID
Variables.Description: Used for precomputed visibility

Variables.Name: NumInstanceVisibilityVolumes
Variables.Description: Used for precomputed visibility

Variables.Name: bForceSyncPSO
Variables.Description:
#if ALLOW_FORCE_SYNC_CREATE_PSO
	  Force this material to link PSO synchronously (on iOS).
	  It avoids popping when the material is not suitable for async linking but may introduce stutters.
	  remove for IG

Variables.Name: OverrideCylinderMaxDrawHeight
Variables.Description: Used if [r.CylinderMaxDrawHeight] is not zero, override [r.CylinderMaxDrawHeight] global setting

Variables.Name: BoundsScale
Variables.Description:
Scales the bounds of the object.
	  This is useful when using World Position Offset to animate the vertices of the object outside of its bounds.
	  Warning: Increasing the bounds of an object will reduce performance and shadow quality!
	  Currently only used by StaticMeshComponent and SkeletalMeshComponent.

Variables.Name: OCBoundsExtent
Variables.Description: ROC Extent the bounds a few pixels during depth test.

Variables.Name: LastSubmitTime
Variables.Description: Last time the component was submitted for rendering (called FScene::AddPrimitive).

Variables.Name: LastRenderTime
Variables.Description:
The value of WorldSettings->TimeSeconds for the frame when this component was last rendered.  This is written
	  from the render thread, which is up to a frame behind the game thread, so you should allow this time to
	  be at least a frame behind the game thread's world time before you consider the actor non-visible.

Variables.Name: MoveIgnoreComponents
Variables.Description:
Set of components to ignore during component sweeps in MoveComponent().
	 These components will be ignored when this component moves or updates overlaps.
	 The other components may also need to be told to do the same when they move.
	 Does not affect movement of this component when simulating physics.
	 @see IgnoreComponentWhenMoving()

Variables.Name: BodyInstance
Variables.Description: Physics scene information for this component, holds a single rigid body with multiple shapes.

Variables.Name: LODParentPrimitive
Variables.Description: LOD parent primitive to draw instead of this one (multiple UPrim's will point to the same LODParent )

Variables.Name: PostPhysicsComponentTick
Variables.Description: Tick function for physics ticking

Variables.Name: IndirectLightingCacheQuality
Variables.Description: Quality of indirect lighting for Movable primitives.  This has a large effect on Indirect Lighting Cache update time.

Variables.Name: bEnableAutoLODGeneration
Variables.Description: If true, and if World setting has bEnableHierarchicalLOD equal to true, then this component will be included when generating a Proxy mesh for the parent Actor

Variables.Name: bUseMaxLODAsImposter
Variables.Description: Use the Maximum LOD Mesh (imposter) instead of including Mesh data from this component in the Proxy Generation process

Variables.Name: ExcludeForSpecificHLODLevels
Variables.Description: Which specific HLOD levels this component should be excluded from

Variables.Name: bIsVisibilityGridProxy
Variables.Description:
Whether to render the primitive in the early z pass for mobile platform.   
	 If the mesh is visibility grid's proxy

Variables.Name: LpvBiasMultiplier
Variables.Description:
Multiplier used to scale the Light Propagation Volume light injection bias, to reduce light bleeding.
	  Set to 0 for no bias, 1 for default or higher for increased biasing (e.g. for
	  thin geometry such as walls)

Variables.Name: bCoastline
Variables.Description: if true, primitive will be collected as coastline

Functions.Name: IgnoreActorWhenMoving
Functions.Description:
Tells this component whether to ignore collision with all components of a specific Actor when this component is moved.
	  Components on the other Actor may also need to be told to do the same when they move.
	  Does not affect movement of this component when simulating physics.

Functions.Name: CopyArrayOfMoveIgnoreActors
Functions.Description: Returns the list of actors we currently ignore when moving.

Functions.Name: ClearMoveIgnoreActors
Functions.Description: Clear the list of actors we ignore when moving.

Functions.Name: IgnoreComponentWhenMoving
Functions.Description:
Tells this component whether to ignore collision with another component when this component is moved.
	 The other components may also need to be told to do the same when they move.
	 Does not affect movement of this component when simulating physics.

Functions.Name: CopyArrayOfMoveIgnoreComponents
Functions.Description: Returns the list of actors we currently ignore when moving.

Functions.Name: ClearMoveIgnoreComponents
Functions.Description: Clear the list of components we ignore when moving.

Functions.Name: IsOverlappingComponent
Functions.Description: Check whether this component is overlapping another component.

Params.Name: OtherComp
Params.Description: Component to test this component against.

Return.Description: Whether this component is overlapping another component.

Functions.Name: IsOverlappingActor
Functions.Description: Check whether this component is overlapping any component of the given Actor.

Params.Name: Other
Params.Description: Actor to test this component against.

Return.Description: Whether this component is overlapping any component of the given Actor.

Functions.Name: GetOverlappingActors
Functions.Description: Returns a list of actors that this component is overlapping.

Params.Name: OverlappingActors
Params.Description: [out] Returned list of overlapping actors

Params.Name: ClassFilter
Params.Description: [optional] If set, only returns actors of this class or subclasses

Functions.Name: GetOverlappingComponents
Functions.Description: Returns list of components this component is overlapping.

Functions.Name: SetBoundsScale
Functions.Description: Scale the bounds of this object, used for frustum culling. Useful for features like WorldPositionOffset.

Functions.Name: GetMaterial
Functions.Description: Returns the material used by the element at the specified index

Params.Name: ElementIndex
Params.Description: - The element to access the material of.

Return.Description: the material used by the indexed element of this mesh.

Functions.Name: SetMaterial
Functions.Description: Changes the material applied to an element of the mesh.

Params.Name: ElementIndex
Params.Description: - The element to access the material of.

Return.Description: the material used by the indexed element of this mesh.

Functions.Name: SetMaterialByName
Functions.Description: Changes the material applied to an element of the mesh.

Params.Name: MaterialSlotName
Params.Description: - The slot name to access the material of.

Return.Description: the material used by the indexed element of this mesh.

Functions.Name: CreateAndSetMaterialInstanceDynamic
Functions.Description: Creates a Dynamic Material Instance for the specified element index.  The parent of the instance is set to the material being replaced.

Params.Name: ElementIndex
Params.Description: - The index of the skin to replace the material for. If invalid, the material is unchanged and NULL is returned.

Functions.Name: CreateAndSetMaterialInstanceDynamicFromMaterial
Functions.Description: Creates a Dynamic Material Instance for the specified element index.  The parent of the instance is set to the material being replaced.

Params.Name: ElementIndex
Params.Description: - The index of the skin to replace the material for. If invalid, the material is unchanged and NULL is returned.

Functions.Name: CreateDynamicMaterialInstance
Functions.Description: Creates a Dynamic Material Instance for the specified element index, optionally from the supplied material.

Params.Name: ElementIndex
Params.Description: - The index of the skin to replace the material for. If invalid, the material is unchanged and NULL is returned.

Functions.Name: GetMaterialFromCollisionFaceIndex
Functions.Description: Try and retrieve the material applied to a particular collision face of mesh. Used with face index returned from collision trace.

Params.Name: FaceIndex
Params.Description: Face index from hit result that was hit by a trace

Params.Name: SectionIndex
Params.Description: Section of the mesh that the face belongs to

Return.Description: Material applied to section that the hit face belongs to

Functions.Name: GetWalkableSlopeOverride
Functions.Description: Returns the slope override struct for this component.

Functions.Name: SetWalkableSlopeOverride
Functions.Description: Sets a new slope override for this component instance.

Functions.Name: SetSimulatePhysics
Functions.Description:
Sets whether or not a single body should use physics simulation, or should be 'fixed' (kinematic).
	 	Note that if this component is currently attached to something, beginning simulation will detach it.

Params.Name: bSimulate
Params.Description: New simulation state for single body

Functions.Name: SetLockedAxis
Functions.Description: Sets the constraint mode of the component.

Functions.Name: SetConstraintMode
Functions.Description: Sets the constraint mode of the component.

Params.Name: ConstraintMode
Params.Description: The type of constraint to use.

Functions.Name: AddImpulse
Functions.Description: Add an impulse to a single rigid body. Good for one time instant burst.

Params.Name: Impulse
Params.Description: Magnitude and direction of impulse to apply.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply impulse to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bVelChange
Params.Description: If true, the Strength is taken as a change in velocity instead of an impulse (ie. mass will have no affect).

Functions.Name: AddAngularImpulse
Functions.Description: Add an angular impulse to a single rigid body. Good for one time instant burst.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply angular impulse to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bVelChange
Params.Description: If true, the Strength is taken as a change in angular velocity instead of an impulse (ie. mass will have no effect).

Functions.Name: AddAngularImpulseInRadians
Functions.Description: Add an angular impulse to a single rigid body. Good for one time instant burst.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply angular impulse to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bVelChange
Params.Description: If true, the Strength is taken as a change in angular velocity instead of an impulse (ie. mass will have no effect).

Functions.Name: AddAngularImpulseInDegrees
Functions.Description: Add an angular impulse to a single rigid body. Good for one time instant burst.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply angular impulse to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bVelChange
Params.Description: If true, the Strength is taken as a change in angular velocity instead of an impulse (ie. mass will have no effect).

Functions.Name: AddImpulseAtLocation
Functions.Description: Add an impulse to a single rigid body at a specific location.

Params.Name: Impulse
Params.Description: Magnitude and direction of impulse to apply.

Params.Name: Location
Params.Description: Point in world space to apply impulse at.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of bone to apply impulse to. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: AddRadialImpulse
Functions.Description: Add an impulse to all rigid bodies in this component, radiating out from the specified position.

Params.Name: Origin
Params.Description: Point of origin for the radial impulse blast, in world space

Params.Name: Radius
Params.Description: Size of radial impulse. Beyond this distance from Origin, there will be no affect.

Params.Name: Strength
Params.Description: Maximum strength of impulse applied to body.

Params.Name: Falloff
Params.Description: Allows you to control the strength of the impulse as a function of distance from Origin.

Params.Name: bVelChange
Params.Description: If true, the Strength is taken as a change in velocity instead of an impulse (ie. mass will have no affect).

Functions.Name: AddForce
Functions.Description:
Add a force to a single rigid body.
	   This is like a 'thruster'. Good for adding a burst over some (non zero) time. Should be called every frame for the duration of the force.

Params.Name: Force
Params.Description: Force vector to apply. Magnitude indicates strength of force.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply force to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bAccelChange
Params.Description: If true, Force is taken as a change in acceleration instead of a physical force (i.e. mass will have no affect).

Functions.Name: AddForce_AssumesLocked
Functions.Description:
Add a force to a single rigid body.
   This is like a 'thruster'. Good for adding a burst over some (non zero) time. Should be called every frame for the duration of the force.
 
 	Notice: AssumesLocked   yufeiili 未加锁版本

Params.Name: Force
Params.Description: Force vector to apply. Magnitude indicates strength of force.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply force to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bAccelChange
Params.Description: If true, Force is taken as a change in acceleration instead of a physical force (i.e. mass will have no affect).

Functions.Name: AddForceAtLocation
Functions.Description:
Add a force to a single rigid body at a particular location in world space.
	   This is like a 'thruster'. Good for adding a burst over some (non zero) time. Should be called every frame for the duration of the force.

Params.Name: Force
Params.Description: Force vector to apply. Magnitude indicates strength of force.

Params.Name: Location
Params.Description: Location to apply force, in world space.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply force to. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: AddForceAtLocation_AssumesLocked
Functions.Description:
Add a force to a single rigid body at a particular location in world space.
   This is like a 'thruster'. Good for adding a burst over some (non zero) time. Should be called every frame for the duration of the force.
 
 	Notice: AssumesLocked   yufeiili 未加锁版本

Params.Name: Force
Params.Description: Force vector to apply. Magnitude indicates strength of force.

Params.Name: Location
Params.Description: Location to apply force, in world space.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply force to. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: AddForceAtLocationLocal
Functions.Description:
Add a force to a single rigid body at a particular location. Both Force and Location should be in body space.
	   This is like a 'thruster'. Good for adding a burst over some (non zero) time. Should be called every frame for the duration of the force.

Params.Name: Force
Params.Description: Force vector to apply. Magnitude indicates strength of force.

Params.Name: Location
Params.Description: Location to apply force, in component space.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply force to. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: AddRadialForce
Functions.Description: Add a force to all bodies in this component, originating from the supplied world-space location.

Params.Name: Origin
Params.Description: Origin of force in world space.

Params.Name: Radius
Params.Description: Radius within which to apply the force.

Params.Name: Strength
Params.Description: Strength of force to apply.

Params.Name: Falloff
Params.Description: Allows you to control the strength of the force as a function of distance from Origin.

Params.Name: bAccelChange
Params.Description: If true, Strength is taken as a change in acceleration instead of a physical force (i.e. mass will have no affect).

Functions.Name: AddTorque
Functions.Description: Add a torque to a single rigid body.

Params.Name: Torque
Params.Description: Torque to apply. Direction is axis of rotation and magnitude is strength of torque.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply torque to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bAccelChange
Params.Description: If true, Torque is taken as a change in angular acceleration instead of a physical torque (i.e. mass will have no effect).

Functions.Name: AddTorqueInRadians
Functions.Description: Add a torque to a single rigid body.

Params.Name: Torque
Params.Description: Torque to apply. Direction is axis of rotation and magnitude is strength of torque.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply torque to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bAccelChange
Params.Description: If true, Torque is taken as a change in angular acceleration instead of a physical torque (i.e. mass will have no effect).

Functions.Description:
Add a torque to a single rigid body.
	 	assumesLocked yufeiii 未加锁版本

Params.Name: Torque
Params.Description: Torque to apply. Direction is axis of rotation and magnitude is strength of torque.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply torque to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bAccelChange
Params.Description: If true, Torque is taken as a change in angular acceleration instead of a physical torque (i.e. mass will have no effect).

Functions.Name: AddTorqueInDegrees
Functions.Description: Add a torque to a single rigid body.

Params.Description: Torque to apply. Direction is axis of rotation and magnitude is strength of torque.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply torque to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bAccelChange
Params.Description: If true, Torque is taken as a change in angular acceleration instead of a physical torque (i.e. mass will have no effect).

Functions.Name: AddTorqueInDegrees_AssumesLocked
Functions.Description:
Add a torque to a single rigid body.
	 	Notice: AssumesLocked   yufeiili 未加锁版本

Params.Name: bAccelChange

Functions.Name: SetPhysicsLinearVelocity
Functions.Description:
Set the linear velocity of a single body.
	 	This should be used cautiously - it may be better to use AddForce or AddImpulse.

Params.Name: NewVel
Params.Description: New linear velocity to apply to physics.

Params.Name: bAddToCurrent
Params.Description: If true, NewVel is added to the existing velocity of the body.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to modify velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: GetPhysicsLinearVelocity
Functions.Description: Get the linear velocity of a single body.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to get velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: GetPhysicsLinearVelocityAtPoint
Functions.Description: Get the linear velocity of a point on a single body.

Params.Name: Point
Params.Description: Point is specified in world space.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to get velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: SetAllPhysicsLinearVelocity
Functions.Description: Set the linear velocity of all bodies in this component.

Params.Name: NewVel
Params.Description: New linear velocity to apply to physics.

Params.Name: bAddToCurrent
Params.Description: If true, NewVel is added to the existing velocity of the body.

Functions.Name: SetPhysicsAngularVelocity
Functions.Description:
Set the angular velocity of a single body.
	 	This should be used cautiously - it may be better to use AddTorque or AddImpulse.

Params.Name: NewAngVel
Params.Description: New angular velocity to apply to body, in degrees per second.

Params.Name: bAddToCurrent
Params.Description: If true, NewAngVel is added to the existing angular velocity of the body.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to modify angular velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: SetPhysicsAngularVelocityInRadians
Functions.Description:
Set the angular velocity of a single body.
	 	This should be used cautiously - it may be better to use AddTorque or AddImpulse.

Params.Name: NewAngVel
Params.Description: New angular velocity to apply to body, in radians per second.

Params.Name: bAddToCurrent
Params.Description: If true, NewAngVel is added to the existing angular velocity of the body.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to modify angular velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: SetPhysicsAngularVelocityInDegrees
Functions.Description:
Set the angular velocity of a single body.
	 	This should be used cautiously - it may be better to use AddTorque or AddImpulse.

Params.Description: If true, NewAngVel is added to the existing angular velocity of the body.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to modify angular velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: NewMaxAngVel

Params.Name: bAddToCurrent
Params.Description: If true, NewMaxAngVel is added to the existing maximum angular velocity of the body.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to modify maximum angular velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: BoneName

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to get velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to get velocity of. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: BoneName

Functions.Name: GetCenterOfMass
Functions.Description:
Get the center of mass of a single body. In the case of a welded body this will return the center of mass of the entire welded body (including its parent and children)
	   Objects that are not simulated return (0,0,0) as they do not have COM

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to get center of mass of. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: SetCenterOfMass
Functions.Description:
Set the center of mass of a single body. This will offset the physx-calculated center of mass.
		Note that in the case where multiple bodies are attached together, the center of mass will be set for the entire group.

Params.Name: CenterOfMassOffset
Params.Description: User specified offset for the center of mass of this object, from the calculated location.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to set center of mass of. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: WakeRigidBody
Functions.Description: 'Wake' physics simulation for a single body.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to wake. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: PutRigidBodyToSleep
Functions.Description: Force a single body back to sleep.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to put to sleep. 'None' indicates root body.

Functions.Name: SetNotifyRigidBodyCollision
Functions.Description: Changes the value of bNotifyRigidBodyCollision

Params.Name: bNewNotifyRigidBodyCollision
Params.Description: - The value to assign to bNotifyRigidBodyCollision

Functions.Name: SetOwnerNoSee
Functions.Description: Changes the value of bOwnerNoSee.

Functions.Name: SetOnlyOwnerSee
Functions.Description: Changes the value of bOnlyOwnerSee.

Functions.Name: SetDrawIdeaOutline
Functions.Description: Changes the value of DrawOutline.

Functions.Name: SetIdeaOutlineUseNormalInVertexColor
Functions.Description: Changes whether use the new outline method which uses normal vectors in vertex colors

Functions.Name: SetIdeaOutlineNew
Functions.Description:
Should only be used in  and Home branch for now. This may significantly increase outline cost. Be sure you need this feature before you enable it. 
	 Changes whether use the new outline pass.

Functions.Name: SetIdeaOutlineOcclusionHighlight
Functions.Description: Changes whether use the occlusion highlight

Functions.Name: SetDisableWriteDepthForOcclusionHighlight
Functions.Description: Changes whether to occlude other primitives' highlight. if this is already occlude highlight, it won't write depth and this flag make no use.

Functions.Name: SetIdeaOverrideOutlineAndOcclusion
Functions.Description: Override outline settings to enable both outline and occlusion

Functions.Name: SetDrawHighlight
Functions.Description: Turn onoff the highlight rendering for this primitive

Functions.Name: SetHighlightCanBeOccluded
Functions.Description: Changes whether the highlight mesh of this primitive can be occluded

Functions.Name: OverrideHighlightColor
Functions.Description: Override the highlight color for this primitive

Params.Name: bOverride
Params.Description: - If true, override the highlight color using InHighlightColor. If false, use the default color in HighlightMaterial.

Params.Name: InHighlightColor
Params.Description: - New color used for highlight rendering

Functions.Name: SetDrawDyeing
Functions.Description: Changes the value of DrawDyeing.

Functions.Name: SetDrawDyeingMode
Functions.Description: Changes the value of DrawDyeingMode.

Functions.Name: SetReveiceShadow
Functions.Description: Changes the value of bReveiceShadow.(by jinglei)

Functions.Name: SetCastShadow
Functions.Description: Changes the value of CastShadow.

Functions.Name: SetCastInsetShadow
Functions.Description: Changes the value of CastInsetShadow.

Functions.Name: SetLightAttachmentsAsGroup
Functions.Description: Changes the value of LightAttachmentsAsGroup.

Functions.Name: SetCastPhotonShadow
Functions.Description:
WITH_PHOTON_SHADOW 
	 Set cast photon shadow.

Functions.Name: SetSingleSampleShadowFromStationaryLights
Functions.Description: Changes the value of bSingleSampleShadowFromStationaryLights.

Functions.Name: SetTranslucentSortPriority
Functions.Description: Changes the value of TranslucentSortPriority.

Functions.Name: SetReceivesDecals
Functions.Description: Changes the value of bReceivesDecals.

Functions.Name: SetCollisionEnabled
Functions.Description: Controls what kind of collision is enabled for this body

Functions.Name: SetCollisionProfileName
Functions.Description:
Set Collision Profile Name
	  This function is called by constructors when they set ProfileName
	  This will change current CollisionProfileName to be this, and overwrite Collision Setting

Params.Name: InCollisionProfileName
Params.Description: : New Profile Name

Functions.Name: GetCollisionProfileName
Functions.Description: Get the collision profile name

Functions.Name: SetCollisionObjectType
Functions.Description: Changes the collision channel that this object uses when it moves

Functions.Name: K2_LineTraceComponent
Functions.Description: Perform a line trace against a single component

Functions.Name: SetRenderCustomDepth
Functions.Description: Sets the bRenderCustomDepth property and marks the render state dirty.

Functions.Name: SetCustomDepthStencilValue
Functions.Description: Sets the CustomDepth stencil value (0 - 255) and marks the render state dirty.

Functions.Name: SetCustomDepthStencilWriteMask
Functions.Description: Sets the CustomDepth stencil write mask and marks the render state dirty.

Functions.Name: SetRenderInMainPass
Functions.Description: Sets bRenderInMainPass property and marks the render state dirty.

Functions.Name: IsRenderInMainPass
Functions.Description: Sets bRenderInMainPass property and marks the render state dirty.

Functions.Name: SetRenderInMono
Functions.Description: Sets bRenderInMono property and marks the render state dirty.

Functions.Name: SetForceIBL
Functions.Description: set bForceIBL

Functions.Name: SetForceDynamic
Functions.Description: set bForceDynamic

Functions.Name: SetTwoPassTranslucent
Functions.Description: Changes the value of Two Pass Translucent.

Functions.Name: SetTranslucentDepthWrite
Functions.Description: Changes the value of Translucent Depth Write.

Functions.Name: SetTranslucentDepthWriteInTwoPass
Functions.Description: Changes the value of Translucent Depth Write In Two Pass.

Return.Description: number of material elements in this primitive

Functions.Name: GetClosestPointOnCollision
Functions.Description:
Returns the distance and closest point to the collision surface.
	 Component must have simple collision to be queried for closest point.

Params.Name: Point
Params.Description: World 3D vector

Params.Name: OutPointOnBody
Params.Description: Point on the surface of collision closest to Point

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to set center of mass of. 'None' indicates root body.

Return.Description: Success if returns > 0.f, if returns 0.f, it is either not convex or inside of the point

Functions.Name: GetCollisionEnabled
Functions.Description: Returns the form of collision for this component

Functions.Name: K2_IsCollisionEnabled
Functions.Description: Utility to see if there is any form of collision (query or physics) enabled on this component.

Functions.Name: K2_IsQueryCollisionEnabled
Functions.Description: Utility to see if there is any query collision enabled on this component.

Functions.Name: K2_IsPhysicsCollisionEnabled
Functions.Description: Utility to see if there is any physics collision enabled on this component.

Functions.Name: GetCollisionResponseToChannel
Functions.Description: Gets the response type given a specific channel

Functions.Name: GetCollisionObjectType
Functions.Description: Gets the collision object type

Functions.Name: SetAllPhysicsAngularVelocity
Functions.Description: Set the angular velocity of all bodies in this component.

Params.Name: NewAngVel
Params.Description: New angular velocity to apply to physics, in degrees per second.

Params.Name: bAddToCurrent
Params.Description: If true, NewAngVel is added to the existing angular velocity of all bodies.

Functions.Name: SetAllPhysicsAngularVelocityInDegrees
Functions.Description: Set the angular velocity of all bodies in this component.

Params.Name: NewAngVel
Params.Description: New angular velocity to apply to physics, in degrees per second.

Params.Name: bAddToCurrent
Params.Description: If true, NewAngVel is added to the existing angular velocity of all bodies.

Functions.Name: SetAllPhysicsAngularVelocityInRadians
Functions.Description: Set the angular velocity of all bodies in this component.

Params.Name: NewAngVel
Params.Description: New angular velocity to apply to physics, in radians per second.

Params.Name: bAddToCurrent
Params.Description: If true, NewAngVel is added to the existing angular velocity of all bodies.

Functions.Name: WakeAllRigidBodies
Functions.Description: Ensure simulation is running for all bodies in this component.

Functions.Name: SetEnableGravity
Functions.Description: Enablesdisables whether this component is affected by gravity. This applies only to components with bSimulatePhysics set to true.

Functions.Name: IsGravityEnabled
Functions.Description: Returns whether this component is affected by gravity. Returns always false if the component is not simulated.

Functions.Name: SetLinearDamping
Functions.Description: Sets the linear damping of this component.

Functions.Name: GetLinearDamping
Functions.Description: Returns the linear damping of this component.

Functions.Name: SetAngularDamping
Functions.Description: Sets the angular damping of this component.

Functions.Name: GetAngularDamping
Functions.Description: Returns the angular damping of this component.

Functions.Name: SetMassScale
Functions.Description: Change the mass scale used to calculate the mass of a single physics body

Functions.Name: GetMassScale
Functions.Description: Returns the mass scale used to calculate the mass of a single physics body

Functions.Name: SetAllMassScale
Functions.Description: Change the mass scale used fo all bodies in this component

Functions.Name: SetMassOverrideInKg
Functions.Description:
Override the mass (in Kg) of a single physics body.
		Note that in the case where multiple bodies are attached together, the override mass will be set for the entire group.
		Set the Override Mass to false if you want to reset the body's mass to the auto-calculated physx mass.

Functions.Name: GetMass
Functions.Description: Returns the mass of this component in kg.

Functions.Name: GetInertiaTensor
Functions.Description: Returns the inertia tensor of this component in kg cm^2. The inertia tensor is in local component space.

Functions.Name: ScaleByMomentOfInertia
Functions.Description: Scales the given vector by the world space moment of inertia. Useful for computing the torque needed to rotate an object.

Functions.Name: IsAnyRigidBodyAwake
Functions.Description: Returns if any body in this component is currently awake and simulating.

Functions.Name: SetCollisionResponseToChannel
Functions.Description: Changes a member of the ResponseToChannels container for this PrimitiveComponent.

Functions.Name: SetCollisionResponseToAllChannels
Functions.Description: Changes all ResponseToChannels container for this PrimitiveComponent. to be NewResponse

Functions.Name: SetPhysMaterialOverride
Functions.Description:
Changes the current PhysMaterialOverride for this component.
	 	Note that if physics is already running on this component, this will _not_ alter its massinertia etc,
	 	it will only change its surface properties like friction.

Functions.Name: SetCullDistance
Functions.Description: Changes the value of CullDistance.

Params.Name: NewCullDistance
Params.Description: - The value to assign to CullDistance.

Params.Name: EnableIncrease
Params.Description: - Whether or not to increase the cull distance if it is greater than the current cull distance.

Functions.Name: CanCharacterStepUp
Functions.Description:
Return true if the given Pawn can step up onto this component.
	  This controls whether they can try to step up on it when they bump in to it, not whether they can walk on it after landing on it.

Params.Name: Pawn
Params.Description: the Pawn that wants to step onto this component.

Delegate.Name: OnComponentHit
Delegate.Description:
Event called when a component hits (or is hit by) something solid. This could happen due to things like Character movement, using Set Location with 'sweep' enabled, or physics simulation.
	 	For events when objects overlap (e.g. walking into a trigger) see the 'Overlap' event.

Delegate.Name: OnComponentBeginOverlap
Delegate.Description:
Event called when something starts to overlaps this component, for example a player walking into a trigger.
	 	For events when objects have a blocking collision, for example a player hitting a wall, see 'Hit' events.

Delegate.Name: OnComponentEndOverlap
Delegate.Description: Event called when something stops overlapping this component

Delegate.Name: OnComponentWake
Delegate.Description: Event called when the underlying physics objects is woken up

Delegate.Name: OnComponentSleep
Delegate.Description: Event called when the underlying physics objects is put to sleep

Delegate.Name: OnComponentCollisionSettingsChangedEvent
Delegate.Description: Event called when collision settings change for this component.

Delegate.Name: OnBeginCursorOver
Delegate.Description: Event called when the mouse cursor is moved over this component and mouse over events are enabled in the player controller

Delegate.Name: OnEndCursorOver
Delegate.Description: Event called when the mouse cursor is moved off this component and mouse over events are enabled in the player controller

Delegate.Name: OnClicked
Delegate.Description: Event called when the left mouse button is clicked while the mouse is over this component and click events are enabled in the player controller

Delegate.Name: OnReleased
Delegate.Description: Event called when the left mouse button is released while the mouse is over this component click events are enabled in the player controller

Delegate.Name: OnInputTouchBegin
Delegate.Description: Event called when a touch input is received over this component when touch events are enabled in the player controller

Delegate.Name: OnInputTouchEnd
Delegate.Description: Event called when a touch input is released over this component when touch events are enabled in the player controller

Delegate.Name: OnInputTouchEnter
Delegate.Description: Event called when a finger is moved over this component when touch over events are enabled in the player controller

Delegate.Name: OnInputTouchLeave
Delegate.Description: Event called when a finger is moved off this component when touch over events are enabled in the player controller
